IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript:
To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather. To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work in your area. Our contractors need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely cut trees away from the overhead cables. By carefully planning this work in advance, we have been able to minimise the duration of the power interruption. This essential work will cover approximately 200 metres of tree cutting across 15 sections of the overhead network.
Doing this helps reduce the risk of future unplanned power cuts and keeps your electricity supply safe and reliable as we appreciate your area has been impacted with unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. This work is taking place at Hill Park Farm.

Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Bev at 07:26 - We can now confirm that all power in the Ingestre Road area should be back on. The issue was caused by an unexpected fault with some of our network equipment, which our engineers have now replaced.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Bev at 07:19 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Talie at 06:49 - Some customers have told us that they want to be notified about a power cut before it happens. We're sorry we couldn't give you advance notice about this power cut. The issue happened suddenly and without warning, leaving us with no time to alert customers.
Our electricity network is regularly inspected and maintained to help prevent outages, but occasionally an unexpected fault can lead to an unplanned power cut. It's similar to a car that's been fully serviced and passed its MOT but still breaks down without warning.
Thank you for your understanding and we will keep you updated about todays outage until you are restored.

It's quite common for power to be restored briefly and go off again; this is our engineers using testing equipment to energise different sections of our network - this helps to narrow down any possible issues.

Our cabling network is often broken down by smaller sites, and our staff may also check these to test for any problems, and they may also walk the cabling route to see if there are any signs of malfunction that have appeared on the surface. Specialist engineers from our control room will continue to divert power remotely, so your power may come back on at any point. We're currently estimating power will be on between 09:30-10:30, but this may change depending on what our staff find. Power back on? To stop receiving texts, follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Otherwise, we'll keep you updated.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.

Steps.Message: Sent by Talie at 08:27 - Thanks for bearing with us over today, I just wanted to give you the latest from our engineers. Our staff have now confirmed that our substation equipment has operated due to an underground cable fault which we'll need to pinpoint, excavate, and repair. We're sending specialist teams and equipment to site, and it's possible they will still be able to restore your power within the current time frame using other methods. However, once they've assessed further, we'll let you know if the time frame needs to change. For more information about this type of fault; here's a short video https://UKPN.short.gy/UG - I'll keep you updated.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Our staff have now arrived on site in the College Road area. Due to there being multiple power cuts over a short space of time, our engineers are going to start their tests at the nearby substation.

Our current time frame is between 11:30 and 12:30; this is an estimate based on them checking the state of our network, and attempting to restore your power.

It's possible that the underground cabling fault we believe has been causing these power cuts has reach a point where they can't safely replace our substation equipment. If this happens we'll need to arrange further teams and equipment, so I'll let you know more news as soon as our staff update us. Sent by Talie at 08:18.
